---
title: 'HOT AND COLD, but not lukewarm!'
date: '2011-12-05T15:19:00.0000000+00:00'
draft: true
blogger_blog: 'Darrell Wolfe, Storyteller'
blogger_status: 'LIVE'
blogger_post_id: '8278325343844498476'
blogger_filename: '/2011/12/hot-and-cold-but-not-lukewarm.html'
blogger_source: 'takeout-20260223T235336Z-3-001.zip:Takeout/Blogger/Blogs/Darrell Wolfe, Storyteller/feed.atom'
categories:
  - 'study'
  - 'Daily Reading'
---

<b>December 5th, 2011&nbsp;</b><br /><br /><b>COME:</b><br /><b><a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/nkjv/revelation/3.html" target="_blank">Revelations 3</a></b><br /><br /><span class="versetext" id="re3-7" style="display: inline; font-family: arial; font-size: 14px; line-height: 20px; margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;"><span class="versenum" style="font-weight: bold; margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 3px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;">7</span>&nbsp;"And to the angel of the church in Philadelphia write,'These things says He who is holy, He who is true, "He who has the key of David, <b>He who opens and no one shuts, and shuts and no one opens"</b>:&nbsp;<a href="http://draft.blogger.com/blogger.g?blogID=6583916183087899714" name="c"></a></span><span class="versetext" id="re3-8" style="background-color: white; display: inline; font-family: arial; font-size: 14px; line-height: 20px; margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;"><span class="versenum" style="font-weight: bold; margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 3px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;">8</span>&nbsp;I know your works. See, I have set before you an open door, and no one can shut it;&nbsp;<a href="http://draft.blogger.com/blogger.g?blogID=6583916183087899714" name="d"></a>for you have a little strength, have kept My word, and have not denied My name.&nbsp;</span><br /><br /><b>(When God opens a door no man can shut it! Glory!)</b><br /><br /><b>COME:</b><br /><span class="versetext" id="re3-15" style="display: inline; font-family: arial; font-size: 14px; line-height: 20px; margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;"><span class="versenum" style="font-weight: bold; margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 3px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;">15</span>&nbsp;I know your works, that you are neither cold nor hot. <b>I could wish you were cold or hot</b>.&nbsp;</span><span class="versetext" id="re3-16" style="background-color: white; display: inline; font-family: arial; font-size: 14px; line-height: 20px; margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;"><span class="versenum" style="font-weight: bold; margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 3px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;">16</span>&nbsp;So then,<b> because you are lukewarm, and neither cold nor hot,<a href="http://draft.blogger.com/blogger.g?blogID=6583916183087899714" name="g"></a>I will vomit you out of My mouth</b>.&nbsp;</span><span class="versetext" id="re3-17" style="background-color: white; display: inline; font-family: arial; font-size: 14px; line-height: 20px; margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;"><span class="versenum" style="font-weight: bold; margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 3px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;">17</span>&nbsp;Because you say, 'I am rich, have become wealthy, and have need of nothing'--and do not know that you are wretched, miserable, poor, blind, and naked--&nbsp;</span><span class="versetext" id="re3-18" style="background-color: white; display: inline; font-family: arial; font-size: 14px; line-height: 20px; margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;"><span class="versenum" style="font-weight: bold; margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 3px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;">18</span>&nbsp;I counsel you to buy from Me gold refined in the fire, that you may be rich; and white garments, that you may be clothed, that the shame of your nakedness may not be revealed; and anoint your eyes with eye salve, that you may see.&nbsp;</span><br /><br /><b>HEAR:</b><br /><a href="http://www.padfield.com/2005/laodicea.html" target="_blank">Laodicea </a>was located between the hot springs of&nbsp;<span class="Apple-style-span" style="background-color: white; font-family: Georgia, 'Times New Roman', Times, serif; font-size: 14px;"><a href="http://draft.blogger.com/goog_1045879884">Hierapolis</a></span><span class="Apple-style-span" style="background-color: white; font-family: Georgia, 'Times New Roman', Times, serif; font-size: 14px;"><a href="http://www.padfield.com/2005/hierapolis.html" target="_blank">&nbsp;</a>and the cold waters of&nbsp;</span><span class="Apple-style-span" style="background-color: white; font-family: Georgia, 'Times New Roman', Times, serif; font-size: 14px;"><a href="http://www.padfield.com/2005/colosse.html" target="_blank">Colosse</a>. Laodicea used</span><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-family: Georgia, 'Times New Roman', Times, serif;"><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-size: 14px;">&nbsp;aqueducts to transport water to their city. The hot springs full of minerals cooled to warm on their way to the city. The cold waters warmed and collected minerals on their way to the city. The result was a luke warm&nbsp;sulfur&nbsp;filled water. Periodically the&nbsp;sulfur&nbsp;content would be high enough to produce vomiting in the drinker.&nbsp;</span></span><br /><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-family: Georgia, 'Times New Roman', Times, serif;"><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-size: 14px;"><br /></span></span><br /><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-family: Georgia, 'Times New Roman', Times, serif;"><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-size: 14px;"><b>HOT </b>Springs are&nbsp;naturally&nbsp;hot water, usually heated by underground water being close to lava flows. They are full of minerals and healthy for the body. They have a healing and restorative nature. Desert Hot Springs, CA was a place that had natural hot springs. It would have been beautiful if the drug culture hadn't moved in there. Typically these are bathed in.&nbsp;</span></span><br /><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-family: Georgia, 'Times New Roman', Times, serif;"><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-size: 14px;"><br /></span></span><br /><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-family: Georgia, 'Times New Roman', Times, serif;"><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-size: 14px;"><b>COLD </b>Springs are naturally cold waters, usually good for drinking. They have a cool refreshing nature.</span></span><br /><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-family: Georgia, 'Times New Roman', Times, serif;"><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-size: 14px;"><br /></span></span><br /><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-family: Georgia, 'Times New Roman', Times, serif;"><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-size: 14px;"><b>LUKEWARM </b>water was mixed, old, and useless for anything but washing pots and pans. It is full of toxins that make it undrinkable and cause vomiting.&nbsp;</span></span><br /><span class="Apple-style-span" style="background-color: white; font-family: Georgia, 'Times New Roman', Times, serif; font-size: 14px;"><br /></span><br /><span class="Apple-style-span" style="background-color: white; font-family: Georgia, 'Times New Roman', Times, serif; font-size: 14px;">Both of these are GOOD! They are both gifts of God. Some of us have a calling in life to be HOT for God. We brings heat, healing, and restoration to a people weary of the battles of life. Some of us have a calling to be COLD for God. We bring cool refreshing restoration to people thirsty for God's presence. Most of us will function in both of these things for different situations.&nbsp;</span><br /><span class="Apple-style-span" style="background-color: white; font-family: Georgia, 'Times New Roman', Times, serif; font-size: 14px;"><br /></span><br /><span class="Apple-style-span" style="background-color: white; font-family: Georgia, 'Times New Roman', Times, serif; font-size: 14px;">To be lukewarm is to be without use and cause sickness around you. You are neither hot with healing or cold with refreshing. You are useless to God.&nbsp;</span><br /><span class="Apple-style-span" style="background-color: white; font-family: Georgia, 'Times New Roman', Times, serif; font-size: 14px;"><b>DO:</b></span><br /><span class="Apple-style-span" style="background-color: white; font-family: Georgia, 'Times New Roman', Times, serif; font-size: 14px;">Stir Up the gift of God in you... become HOT AND COLD for God again!&nbsp;</span><br /><span class="Apple-style-span" style="background-color: white; font-family: Georgia, 'Times New Roman', Times, serif; font-size: 14px;"><br /></span><br /><span class="Apple-style-span" style="background-color: white; font-family: Georgia, 'Times New Roman', Times, serif; font-size: 14px;"><b>PRAYER:</b></span><br /><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-family: Georgia, 'Times New Roman', Times, serif;"><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-size: 14px;">Father, help me recognize the passion that was in me when I started... help me see how to reignite that passion!</span></span><br /><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-family: Georgia, 'Times New Roman', Times, serif;"><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-size: 14px;"><br /></span></span><br /><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-family: Georgia, 'Times New Roman', Times, serif;"><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-size: 14px;"><a href="http://darrellgwolfe.blogspot.com/2011/12/passion.html" target="_blank">See Also: Passion</a></span></span><br /><span class="Apple-style-span" style="background-color: white; font-family: Georgia, 'Times New Roman', Times, serif; font-size: 14px;"><br /></span><br /><span class="Apple-style-span" style="background-color: white; font-family: Georgia, 'Times New Roman', Times, serif; font-size: 14px;"><b>DW</b></span><br /><a href="http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Laodicean_Church">http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Laodicean_Church</a><br /><br /><a href="http://www.bibleplaces.com/laodicea.htm">http://www.bibleplaces.com/laodicea.htm</a><br /><br /><a href="http://www.padfield.com/2005/laodicea.html">http://www.padfield.com/2005/laodicea.html</a><br /><br /><a href="http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Laodicea_on_the_Lycus">http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Laodicea_on_the_Lycus</a>
